calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Approving And Authorizing Payment Of Trustee Fee
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Beverly Tafelski-Lavin for Glenn Lerner.

The Court addressed the fees stating that it likes to look at the hours and noted that Mr. Lerner's argument is that he spent a lot of time but didn't keep time records. Ms. Tafelski-Lavin stated that there was a $20,000,000.00 Trust set up by the Warren Sichel, that Shirley Sichel receives income from that Trust, that Mr. Lerner is very involved in monitoring the performance of the Warren Sichel Trust, and that he feels a responsibility to protect the interests of the beneficiary with regard to the income from that Trust. The Court inquired as to why Mr. Lerner petitioned after five years. Ms. Tafelski-Lavin stated that Mr. Lerner waited because he wanted to see how things went and that Shirley isn't competent to do it so he wanted Court approval. Ms. Tafelski-Lavin addressed the terms of the Trust stating that there is one bequest of $250,000.00 that will go to a cousin, that a good majority of the balance will go to the Veterinary Medicine Center at UC Davis, and that two other individuals will receive small amounts. Ms. Tafelski-Lavin also stated that Shirley is the income beneficiary in her Husband's Trust (Warren Sichel Trust), that it's possible that Shirley didn't leave anything to her Daughter because her Daughter is the beneficiary to the Warren Sichel Trust, that the real property value has gone up, and that the investments went from $7,000,000.00 to $9.500,000.00. Brief discussions are held. Ms. Tafelski-Lavin presented concluding argument in support of the petition stating that Mr. Lerner wants Court approval so that it is "on the record", that Mr. Lerner has a long history with Ms. Sichel and her family, that he has known them for over 50 years, that he has been proactive in getting as much income for her as he can, that he monitors her interests in the other Trust, that he's involved in the investments, that he is managing her income, and that he's been involved in her care including being on call for her doctor since she's estranged from her Daughter. At the conclusion of Ms. Tafelski-Lavin's argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Chan. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Beverly Talfelski-Lavin for Glenn Lerner.

Ms. Lavin requested a one month continuance stating that the accounting was sent to the remainder beneficiaries. The Court stated that it will need time sheets. The Petition for Order Approving and Authorizing Payment of Trustee Fees is continued to January 14, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.
